# S.T.A.N.D Mentorship — Partnerships, Funders, Sponsors & Events

**Research date:** 2026-06-17
**Analyst note:** Nonprofit partnerships & funding research. All sources are public web pages (URLs inline). Confidence levels: **High** = directly stated in an authoritative/primary source; **Medium** = stated in a single secondary source or strongly implied; **Low/Unconfirmed** = mentioned only as "potential," ambiguous, or not corroborated; **NONE FOUND** = actively searched, no evidence located (important for assessing funding maturity).

---

## 0. Organization identity (disambiguation — verified)

This is the correct target organization. Confirmed identity markers:

- **Legal name:** "Stepping Towards A New Destiny Corporation" (S.T.A.N.D = *Strengthening/Stepping Towards A New Destiny*). **EIN 82-1192567.** Registered in **Parkville, MD** (Baltimore County). 501(c)(3) since **April 2017**. — *High.* Source: https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/821192567
- **Founder/Executive Director:** Tigana S. ("Coach") Duncan; born in Trinidad & Tobago; LinkedIn lists him as CEO of "Stepping Towards A New Destiny." — *High.* Sources: https://www.standmentorship.org/about , https://www.linkedin.com/in/tigana-s-duncan-8073ba360
- **History/geography:** Started ~10+ years ago in **Baltimore City**; expanded to **Howard County in 2015**; free youth leadership academies with a **trades focus** (plumbing, electrician, carpentry, coppersmith, barber, CDL, HVAC). — *High.* Sources: https://columbiacommunitycare.org/harnessing-the-potential-within/ , https://www.standmentorship.org/programs
- **Leadership/board (from About page):** ED Tigana S. Duncan; COO **Dr. Tracey Wheatley** (runs *Living Life Unbroken* ministry; launched Synergy Mobile Labs 2023); Board Chair **Shelly L. Burgess**; Secretary **James Wicks**; members **A. Michelle Jackson, Michael Franklin**; Youth Rep **Jonathan E. Smith** (Roland Park Elem/Middle; Kingdom Worship Center). Staff: **Jahlil Jarrett** (Director of Engagement, Howard Co.), **Darryl Jeffries Jr.** (Program Director, Howard Co.; teaches at Wilde Lake Middle School). — *High.* Source: https://www.standmentorship.org/about

> **Funding-maturity flag:** STAND's own 501(c)(3) reports such low gross receipts that it files **only the 990-N e-postcard** (no full Form 990 on file with ProPublica; <$50k threshold). Its major Howard County funding and program delivery run **through / alongside Columbia Community Care** (see below). This is an early-stage funding profile despite a long operating history.

---

## 1. Partnerships / institutional relationships

### Columbia Community Care (CCC) — PRIMARY institutional partner / umbrella host
- STAND operates as a youth program **under/with Columbia Community Care** in Howard County. CCC's ED is **Erika Strauss Chavarria**. STAND received **CCC's "Outstanding Partnership Award 2024."** CCC acts as a fiscal sponsor for partner projects and is named jointly with STAND in county funding. — *High.* Sources: https://columbiacommunitycare.org/what-we-do/youth/s-t-a-n-d/ , https://columbiacommunitycare.org/who-we-are/ , https://columbiacommunitycare.org/summer-youth-program-prevents-crime-and-strengthens-self-worth/
- Listed memberships/affiliations on CCC's STAND page: **Maryland Nonprofits, Maryland Food Bank, GuideStar/Candid.** — *Medium.* Source: https://columbiacommunitycare.org/what-we-do/youth/s-t-a-n-d/

### PUSH (Persevere Until Success Happens) — sibling program / co-deliverer
- Led by **Saquan Maxwell.** STAND + PUSH jointly ran the **"United We STAND" 6-week summer program (2025)**; the two are consistently funded together by Howard County. — *High.* Source: https://columbiacommunitycare.org/summer-youth-program-prevents-crime-and-strengthens-self-worth/

### Howard County Government — deepest institutional relationship
- **Youth Engagement Strategy (YES!) Council:** Tigana Duncan is a member of County Executive **Calvin Ball's** 27-member council (est. March 31, 2025) addressing youth violence. STAND named as a funded youth program. — *High.* Sources: https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News033125 , https://hoodline.com/2025/04/howard-county-executive-calvin-ball-launches-yes-council-to-combat-youth-violence-and-bolster-community-relations/
- **The Source community center:** County secured a permanent home there for "PUSH & STAND." — *High.* Source: https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News040924
- **Criminal-justice partners:** STAND "partners with the Howard County court system, the State's Attorney, Sheriff's Department, local politicians and community leaders" to reduce youth recidivism (court-referred youth in summer program). — *High.* Sources: https://columbiacommunitycare.org/summer-youth-program-prevents-crime-and-strengthens-self-worth/ , https://www.cbsnews.com/baltimore/news/columbia-mall-violence-howard-maryland-emmetson-robertson-mcray/
- *(Funding detail in Section 2.)*

### Schools & education
- **Wilde Lake Middle School (HCPSS):** Confirmed **program site** — STAND runs its Howard County program out of Wilde Lake Middle, led by Darryl Jeffries Jr. (who teaches there). — *High* as a site; no evidence of a formal district-wide MOU. Sources: https://www.cbsnews.com/baltimore/news/columbia-mall-violence-howard-maryland-emmetson-robertson-mcray/ , https://www.standmentorship.org/about
- **Howard County Public School System (HCPSS), Howard Community College, Columbia Association:** Named only as **"potential collaborators"** in the YES! Council announcement — **no confirmed formal partnership.** — *Low/Unconfirmed.* Source: https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News033125
- **Frostburg State University:** **NONE FOUND.** No evidence of any relationship with STAND despite targeted searches.
- **Baltimore City Schools / Baltimore County Schools:** **NONE FOUND** as institutional partners (org has Baltimore roots but no named school partnership surfaced).

### Faith partners
- **Living Life Unbroken** ministry — run by COO Dr. Tracey Wheatley (internal leadership tie). — *High.* Source: https://www.standmentorship.org/about
- **Kingdom Worship Center** — affiliation of board youth rep / leadership. — *Medium.* Source: https://www.standmentorship.org/about
- **Celebration Church** (Pastor Matthew Quainoo) sits on the YES! Council but is **not** a confirmed STAND partner. — *Low.* Source: https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News033125

### Trades / workforce partners
- STAND advertises trades training (plumbing, electrician, carpentry, coppersmith, barber, CDL, HVAC) and "firehouse visit (apprenticeship opportunities)" during the summer program — but **NO named training providers, unions, apprenticeship programs, HVAC/CDL/electrical schools, or barber schools were found.** Programs reference "a network of trained mentors and volunteers" only. — **NONE FOUND (named partners).** Sources: https://www.standmentorship.org/programs , https://columbiacommunitycare.org/summer-youth-program-prevents-crime-and-strengthens-self-worth/

### Pro sports teams (Ravens / Orioles) — IMPORTANT NEGATIVE
- The org lists a **"Ravens Training Camp" (2023)** and **"S.T.A.N.D Orioles Baseball Game Night" (2014)** as past events, but **NO evidence of any formal community partnership** with the Baltimore Ravens or Orioles exists. Ravens training camp is free/public; the Orioles event reads as a group ticket outing. Treat both as **youth outings, not partnerships.** — *Low/None (formal partnership).* Source: https://www.standmentorship.org/past-events

---

## 2. Funders & grants

### Howard County Government — by far the dominant funder
| Funding | Amount | Year | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| County budget allocation for STAND & PUSH (current year) | **$100,000** | FY announced 3/31/2025 | High | https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News033125 |
| Prior county support for STAND & PUSH (cumulative) | **~$250,000** | prior 2 years (~2023–2024) | High | https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News033125 |
| **YEP! (Youth Engagement Programming) grant** — STAND is a named recipient (1 of ~30–33 nonprofits; program total >$1.5M since 2023) | amount not disclosed for STAND | 2023–2025 rounds | High | https://www.cbsnews.com/baltimore/news/columbia-mall-violence-howard-maryland-emmetson-robertson-mcray/ , https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News090325a |

- *Caveat:* County announcements name **"STAND and PUSH" jointly**, and funding is closely tied to **Columbia Community Care**, so the precise split attributable to STAND alone is not published.
- **HoCo STRIVES** ($2M+ umbrella) and **Club Wilde Lake / Boys & Girls Club at HCC** are related county youth investments but **not** confirmed as direct STAND grants. — *Low.* Source: https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News040924

### Black Philanthropy Circle of Howard County (BPCHoCo) — a fund of the Community Foundation of Howard County
- **BPCHoCo's first-ever grant** was awarded to the **S.T.A.N.D. Mentorship Program**, presented to Tigana Duncan at BPCHoCo's 2nd annual *College Connections & Conversations* event. Awarded **December 2025** (publicized Jan 2026). **Dollar amount not disclosed.** BPCHoCo founded 2023; supports the African diaspora in Howard County. — *High* (grant exists); *amount unknown.* Sources: https://cfhoco.org/black-philanthropy-circle-of-howard-county-awards-first-ever-grant-to-s-t-a-n-d-mentorship-program/ , https://www.bpchoco.org/
- **Note:** STAND was **NOT** among the 50 recipients of CFHoCo's main Jan-2025 competitive round ($185,500 total) — its CFHoCo money came specifically via the Black Philanthropy Circle fund. Source: https://thedailyrecord.com/2025/01/08/community-foundation-of-howard-county-awards-grants/

### Howard County Citizens Association (HCCA) — small donor
- **HCCA donated $500** to STAND Mentorship (date not specified). — *High* (small). Source: https://howardcountyhcca.org/

### Foundations & state grants actively searched — NONE FOUND
The following were specifically searched against STAND / "Stepping Towards a New Destiny" / Tigana Duncan and produced **NO evidence of any grant**:
- **Horizon Foundation of Howard County** — NONE FOUND
- **Community Foundation of Howard County** main competitive grant round — NONE FOUND (only the BPCHoCo fund, above)
- **Baltimore Community Foundation** — NONE FOUND
- **Harry & Jeanette Weinberg Foundation** — NONE FOUND
- **France-Merrick Foundation** — NONE FOUND
- **Abell Foundation** — NONE FOUND
- **T. Rowe Price Foundation** — NONE FOUND
- **CareFirst** — NONE FOUND
- **United Way of Central Maryland** — NONE FOUND
- **Maryland Governor's Office of Crime Prevention & Policy (state youth/violence grants)** — NONE FOUND
- **ARPA youth funds (named as such)** — NONE FOUND (county funding source not labeled ARPA in releases)
- **Howard County Community Service Partnership (CSP) grants** — NONE FOUND (STAND's county money is via YEP! + budget allocations, not CSP per available sources)

---

## 3. Sponsors (corporate)

- **NONE FOUND.** No corporate sponsors are named anywhere on the website (home, about, programs, events, donate), in news coverage, or in event write-ups. This is a notable gap and a clear funding-maturity signal.
- Closest item: a "STAND Gear" merch reference on Instagram (self-branded, not a sponsor). Source: https://www.instagram.com/standmentorship/

---

## 4. Fundraising platforms / campaigns

- **Website native donation form** at https://www.standmentorship.org/donate-now — a basic Wix form (name, email, custom amount); **no third-party processor (PayPal/Givebutter/Donorbox/Zeffy/Stripe) is exposed.** — *Medium.*
- **Columbia Community Care** also hosts a donation/campaign link for STAND. — *Medium.* Source: https://columbiacommunitycare.org/what-we-do/youth/s-t-a-n-d/
- **GoFundMe — NONE FOUND. Givebutter — NONE FOUND. Eventbrite — NONE FOUND. Donorbox — NONE FOUND. Facebook fundraisers — NONE FOUND. Galas / golf tournaments / formal fundraising events — NONE FOUND.** Searched directly; no campaigns located.
- Social presence: Facebook (@standmentorship1), Instagram (@standmentorship and @_standmentorship). — *High* (exist); used for awareness, not visible structured fundraising. Sources: https://www.facebook.com/standmentorship1 , https://www.instagram.com/standmentorship/

> **Funding-maturity flag:** No modern crowdfunding/peer-to-peer fundraising infrastructure and no named corporate sponsorships. Revenue appears almost entirely **government (Howard County) + one giving-circle grant + small community donations.**

---

## 5. Events with partners

Website "Past Events" gallery lists 8 events — **all photo galleries with NO sponsor/partner detail on-site** (itself a maturity signal). Source: https://www.standmentorship.org/past-events

| Event | Location / Date | Partners/sponsors found | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| S.T.A.N.D Orioles Baseball Game Night | Baltimore, 2014 | None found (outing, not a partnership) | Low/None |
| Ocean City Summer Trip | Ocean City, MD — May 2017 | None found | None |
| Unity "The Community" Basketball Game (annual Father-Son game) | Baltimore — 2017 (and recurring) | **We Our Us / Bmore Men United** (Dr. Andrey Bundley); Nick Mosby (played); Big Fred Watkins (MC); Mike Battle (food); venue **Robert C. Marshall Recreation Center, Upton**. *Mosby's "Council President" title suggests a post-2020 iteration of the recurring game.* | Medium-High (event partners) |
| Camping Trip | Maryland, 2023 | None found | None |
| Ravens Training Camp | Baltimore, 2023 | None found (public event attendance) | Low/None |
| S.T.A.N.D New York / Philadelphia Trip | NY & PA, 2023 | None found | None |
| Baltimore Workshop | Baltimore, 2024 | None found | None |
| Howard County Workshop | Columbia, MD, 2024 | None found | None |

**"United We STAND" summer program (2025)** — STAND+PUSH under Columbia Community Care; youth got $100/day stipends + meals; field trips to **Hershey Park, Ocean City, paintball, go-karts, trampoline parks, and a firehouse (apprenticeship exposure).** Served 32 court-referred/at-risk/high-achieving teens; reported zero new arrests among court-referred participants. — *High.* Source: https://columbiacommunitycare.org/summer-youth-program-prevents-crime-and-strengthens-self-worth/

---

## 6. Other nonprofit collaborations

- **PUSH (Saquan Maxwell)** — closest peer collaborator (joint program). — *High.*
- **We Our Us / Bmore Men United (Dr. Andrey Bundley)** — Baltimore co-host of the Father-Son/Unity basketball event. — *Medium-High.* Source: https://www.wmar2news.com/news/region/baltimore-city/father-son-basketball-game-brings-community-outside-the-lines
- **Columbia Community Care partner network (peers in the CCC ecosystem):** Community Movie Night, Community Ecology Institute, All This Math, Prime Time 4 Unity, Racial Justice NOW!, Anti-Racist Education Alliance, Women's Giving Circle, Financial Literacy for Latinos, Homewood Center. STAND is a peer/partner within this roster. — *Medium.* Source: https://columbiacommunitycare.org/who-we-are/
- **Maryland Nonprofits / Maryland Food Bank / GuideStar(Candid)** — listed affiliations/memberships. — *Medium.* Source: https://columbiacommunitycare.org/what-we-do/youth/s-t-a-n-d/
- *Possible false positive:* a "STAND – Stepping Towards a New Direction" appears on a national Community Action Partnership showcase — **likely a different initiative** (name variant "Direction" not "Destiny"); not corroborated as this org. — *Low/Disregard.*

---

## 7. Summary assessment (funding maturity)

- **Confirmed funders:** Howard County Government (dominant — YEP! grant + ~$350k in budget allocations for STAND/PUSH over ~3 years); Black Philanthropy Circle of Howard County (first grant, Dec 2025, amount undisclosed); HCCA ($500).
- **Confirmed institutional partners:** Columbia Community Care (umbrella/host), PUSH, Howard County (YES! Council, The Source, courts/State's Attorney/Sheriff), Wilde Lake Middle School (site), We Our Us (event).
- **Notable absences (NONE FOUND, despite targeted search):** every major regional/national foundation (Horizon, Weinberg, Abell, France-Merrick, T. Rowe Price, CareFirst, Baltimore Community Foundation, United Way); state crime-prevention grants; corporate sponsors; crowdfunding/peer-to-peer platforms; formal Ravens/Orioles partnerships; Frostburg State and HCC partnerships.
- **Interpretation:** An emerging, founder-led, very small 501(c)(3) (990-N filer) whose recent growth (2023–2025) is driven almost entirely by **Howard County public dollars channeled with/through Columbia Community Care**, supplemented by one giving-circle grant and minor community gifts. Diversified philanthropic, corporate, and digital-fundraising revenue is essentially undeveloped.
